title: Regional Market Area Mobility Improvements — $3.5M Pedestrian/Bicycle Project Overview
summary: Public Transportation Committee chair Marino L. Nave convenes meeting on the RTC/Regional Market area mobility improvement project, a $3.5M federally and state-funded pedestrian and bicycle infrastructure project. City engineer Marza Malach presents: project is in preliminary design, covering Park Street from Hiawatha Boulevard to NBT Parkway and connecting streets. Improvements include 8-10 foot shared-use paths, new ADA-compliant crosswalks with push buttons, and mid-block crossings near the Regional Transportation Center. Construction estimated spring 2027, potentially delayed by NYS DOT review backlogs. Councilor Nave frames the project as a 20-year advocacy effort for north side residents without vehicles, particularly new Americans who walk to the regional market.

speakers.name: Marino L. Nave
speakers.role: Committee Chair (Public Transportation), Common Councilor District 1

political_signal.description: Councilor Nave has championed north side pedestrian infrastructure for 20 years, framing this as essential for new Americans and residents without vehicles
